reason: Core atomic ops; every other sync primitive composes on these. Wrong here = cascaded unsoundness across ~half the crate. Rubric tier 5.
Classical sync primitive, Verus-verified. Tier 5 per rubric; recent GAP-2 wire-up of signal/broadcast/wait decisions (commit bf8111a) expanded the proof surface — fresh drift risk.
Tier 5 per rubric. Known TOCTOU bug in gale_futex.c shim (issue #16): atomic_get read outside the spinlock — upstream fix 10c974c9d5 not yet ported. High evidence the module class harbours bugs.
Classical sync primitive; heavy proof coverage and deep FFI surface in ffi/src/lib.rs. Tier 5 per rubric.
Scheduler. Known self-IPI race in the gale_sched.c shim (issue #17): signal_pending_ipi() called outside K_SPINLOCK — upstream fix 1666066082 missing. Permanent CPU hang reproducible under QEMU.
Foundational. give_decide/take_decide are the reference pattern followed by every subsequent primitive; proof regressions here ripple. Tier 5 per rubric.
Lock primitive under every kernel critical section. Tier 5 per rubric.
Thread state transitions. Tier 5 per rubric; state-machine soundness is load-bearing for sched/wait_queue/userspace invariants.

Safety-critical subset of thread.c (create/start/abort). State transitions + MAX_THREADS bound; GAP-2 delegations were added recently (gale_thread_create_validate/exit_validate). Promoted from tier 4 because semantics overlap thread.rs.
C-ABI boundary, 275 unsafe occurrences. Every raw-pointer deref and every CONFIG_GALE_KERNEL_* handler lives here. Tier 4 per rubric; unsafe-heavy bumps evidence further.
C-ABI shim (16 unsafe). Coarse-grained locking boundary for a specific overlay path.
Per-CPU thread binding. Safety-critical for SMP partitioning / priority inheritance correctness. Rubric tier 4.
Early boot; invariants not yet established. Rubric tier 4.
Fatal-error path; must terminate cleanly. Rubric tier 4.
ARM Cortex-M fault register decoding. Bug here mis-classifies hardware faults — ASIL-D diagnostic correctness. Promoted from tier 3 per rubric safety note.
Inter-processor interrupt, runs in IRQ context. Rubric tier 4 (interrupt-class).
MMU virtual address space. Only src/ file with raw-pointer arithmetic (addr_of/ptr::). Rubric tier 3 promoted to 4 by the unsafe-bump rule.
ARMv7-M MPU region configuration. Userspace enforcement depends on this being correct; wrong config silently defeats isolation.
6,164 BLE call sites. CVE-2023-5564 (double-free under concurrency) is the exact class for this module — rubric explicitly names it. Tier 3 promoted to 4 by CVE evidence.
SMP state transitions; rubric tier 4 (equivalent to src/smp.rs entry).
Validates spinlock usage (lock-under-lock, ISR-call, etc.). Adjacent to tier-5 spinlock.rs; a broken validator lets the real bug through.
Syscall validation: privilege-boundary code. k_object_access_check / k_object_validate gate every user→kernel transition. Recent missing shim symbol (commit 9b99ef7) shows drift class.
Dynamic thread allocation. Rubric tier 3.
Kernel events with wait queues. Rubric tier 3.
Wait-queue-backed FIFO. Concurrent access; class equivalent to queue.c. Rubric tier 3.
Concurrent allocator. Issue #15 already tracks heap hardening drift in the C shim; the Rust model has known coverage gaps too.
IPC service endpoint state machine. Recently expanded via differential tests; drift from upstream rpmsg backend likely.
Kernel heap. Issue #18 tracks missing k_heap_free_sched_locked variant in the C shim; Rust model is the correctness oracle for that path.
LIFO variant of queue.c. Same tier as fifo/queue.
Mailbox with thread rendezvous. Concurrent, timeout-aware.
Memory domain partitioning for userspace. Rubric tier 3; bug here silently widens access.
Fixed-size block allocator. CVE-2021-3580 (integer overflow) — rubric explicitly names the class.
Variable-size block pool. Issue #18 recursive-lock cascade applies to the C shim.
Ring-buffer-backed message queue. Class equivalent to queue.c; rubric tier 3.
Byte-stream pipe with blocking read/write. Concurrent access patterns; rubric tier 3.
Multi-object wait. Rubric tier 3.
Base queue primitive (k_queue). Used by fifo/lifo/msgq/etc. Rubric tier 3.
Lock-free ring buffer. Concurrent correctness is subtle (producer/consumer invariants). Rubric tier 3.
k_stack kernel object. Class equivalent to queue.c.
ARM Cortex-M SysTick timer. Recently fully Verus-verified (14/14 functions); proof surface expanded — drift risk even with proofs.
Timeout scheduling and tick advancement. Rubric tier 3.
k_timer; period classification + overflow-checked increment.
Timeslice scheduler decision. Tier 3 adjacent to sched.rs.
Wait-queue primitive underlying sem/mutex/condvar/event. Rubric tier 3.
Work queue state machine. Rubric tier 3.
printf formatting; CVE-2022-3806 (stack buffer overflow) is the rubric-named class. Tier 2 kept because the gale model is validation-only and does not do the byte copy.
Errno constants + Result types. Rubric tier 2.
Power management state machine. Decision logic only; no direct hardware register access in this module.
Bounded priority type. Small surface; constraint enforcement.
Thread stack setup validation. Bounded arithmetic over stack sizes; rubric tier 2.
Thread runtime statistics. Mostly accumulators with overflow guards.
Flash-backed KV (Zephyr Memory Storage) sector state machine. Off the critical sync path; separate P1 gaps tracked in issue #8.
Crate root; re-exports only. Rubric tier 1.
